column1: Sentence
CRYPTOLOCKER AND ADMIN
Technical Specifications
PURPOSE
To write a cryptolocker and backend/admin for it taking into account current market realities.
ROLES IN THE SYSTEM
There are the following roles:
- admin (us)
- Advert (partner, registers in our system to earn money)
- victim
- Recoverer (intermediary between the victim and us, plays for the victim)
BUSINESS MODEL AND PAYOUTS
All payments to adverts go in transit through our wallets.
The victim pays to a bitcoin address linked to the system owner's wallet.
From this wallet, the money is sent to the adverts; our commissions are deposited in the wallet.
Thus, the advert does NOT CONTROL the bitcoin addresses that are specified on the landing pages,
and CANNOT specify his addresses and CONTACTS on the landing page.
In general, a landing page must be strictly censored by the system.
When an Advertiser creates a new target, we assign one of our bitcoin addresses to the target.
This address is selected from the pool of addresses (linked to our wallet) set in the system settings.
The system tracks the movement to the addresses assigned to the adverts, and forwards all incoming payments minus the commission on the advert
to the addresses specified by the adverts themselves.
This scheme requires increased security when working with the master wallet.
Only the owner of the system should have access to it.
It's like cryptocurrency exchanges.
Recovery companies have a special role in this business, and it requires specific support in software.
As a rule, recavers ask us for a discount, buy out the anlocker, and sell it to the victim at a nominal price.
Their profit is this discount.
So to support these actions there are the following features:
- private chat and clearing chat history
- commands in chat to hide the anchor and manage the landing page.
The point of them is to hide from the victim the fact that the anchor is available.
BOTH LOCKER
The bot must check its IP against online geo-databases.
PRESENCE OF IP-ADDRESS IN THE CIS ZONE LEADS TO IMMEDIATE TERMINATION AND DELETION FROM DISK!
How to get your external address see p.7.
the bot must ensure its persistence in the system (to be saved between reboots).
https://habr.com/ru/post/425177/
2. the bot should be granted the maximum possible privileges in the system:
The bot must bypass UAC:
https://github.com/hfiref0x/UACME
(working methods are somewhere around mid 30's...).
Do not try it on a personal machine!
It may damage the OS.
4. When installed on the system, the bot must generate an ID.
The bot ID is a string consisting of two dot-separated components.
The first part has the format %MACHINE%-%USER%_XYYYYYYYYY, where
MACHINE - computer name
USER - user name
X - symbol denoting the type of system the client is running on.
(W - windows, L - linux, A - android, M - Mac OS),
YYYYYYYYYY - 3-7 digits containing major-version, minor-version and build of the operating system, if there are any such systems
(for example, for 6.1 build 7600 it will be 617600).
The second part contains 32 random characters 0-9, A-F.
An example of a client id is HOSTNAME-USER_W617600.11223344556677889900AABBCCDDEEFF.
The parameter is not case sensitive.
The bot has the name of its group - a string of six characters, the first three characters are lower case Latin, the rest are numbers.
The bot must have a builder attached to it, flashing its group and other essential parameters into the bot at the layout stage.
The task of the builder is to flash into the bot the resources to be modified without giving access to the source code.
The following scheme is proposed:
- Bilder receives .obj-files as input
- .obj-file containing resources to be modified (group, etc.) is compiled from source (or processed by some utility).
The bot should collect the following information about the environment:
- OS version and build
- list of network interfaces with IP addresses
- external IP address
https://habr.com/ru/company/emercoin/blog/335458/
https://github.com/emercoin/emercoin/blob/master/src/stun.cpp
// list of installed programs
This data is sent to the command server.
The bot gets the address of the command server via Emercoin DNS domain resolving.
Once installed and anchored in the system, the bot starts encrypting files in the system using the following algorithm:
8.1 The encryption algorithm is optionally ChaCha or another streaming asymmetric cipher.
Crypto algorithm requirements:
- streaming (designed for fast stream processing)
- fast
- asymmetric (there are private and public keys)
- there is an open implementation in C.
We do not try to implement crypto algorithms ourselves, we take only ready-made variants!
8.2 The program encrypts files with the public key embedded in it.
After the file is processed, a file with the same name and double extension .txt.crypted is created next to it,
which contains the lending-text.
8.2.1 Take into account that the file extension is signature #1 for antiviruses.
You may want to use standard extensions (.txt) instead of non-standard ones (.crypted, .crypt, etc.) as much as possible.
The program works in one of two modes: fast or full.
In fast mode, only the first megabyte of the file is encrypted.
This is necessary to cover the system quickly.
In full mode, the entire file is encrypted.
8.4 Before starting the program kills processes from the list and stops services from the list.
In case of errors, the program tries to repeat the action three times with an interval of 2 minutes.
Further work does not depend on the result of this step.
The program first processes directories from a special "fast" list - the list of directories,
which must be passed through first.
The program DOES NOT touch files and directories from a special stop list - a list of files that cannot be touched.
The combinations of the quick list and the stop list are handled in this way:
We cover all paths from the quick list that are not on the stop list
8.6.2 If the entire disk is in the stop list, we cover only the paths from the quick list on that disk, without touching the rest of the disk
8.6.3 If the stop list is empty, cover the folders from the quick list first; cover the rest of the files later.
The program encrypts only files with extensions from the list of working extensions; the rest are ignored.
The program deletes files from the special list by overwriting their contents three times:
- first time with constant 0
- second time with constant FF
- third time with random garbage
- the fourth time the file is deleted
The program processes all disks in this way.
All errors during operation are ignored.
Thus, there are the following lists:
List of changeable settings in the program:
Group
encryption key
9.3. list of processes and services
quick list
stop list
9.6. list of working extensions
delete list
9.8. landing page text
9.9. command server address
All these data (except 9.1, 9.9) the program tries to request from the command server via the Tor network, ten attempts, interval 1 minute.
If unsuccessful, the program uses the stored values, periodically resuming attempts to update the settings.
BACKEND'A API
POST /HELLO
HTTP/1.1
The request is executed at program startup (both initial and repeated after reboot).
The content of the HTTP request body is an application/x-www-form-urlencoded container with the following fields:
cid - client ID
group - client group
ip1 - address of the first network interface
ip2 - address of the second network interface
ipN - external IP address
If the bot receives a 406 response code and a Not allowed response, the bot is immediately terminated and removed from disk.
In turn, the backend should issue this code and response if it detects that any of the IP addresses provided in the request
is in the CIS zone.
GET /<cid>/<group>/<setting>
HTTP/1.1
Setup request.
Here
<cid> - client ID
<group> - client group
<setting> - setting, enum of the
key (public encryption key)
services (list of processes and services - separated by semicolons.
If the field has a .exe substring, it is the name of the process.
If not, it is the name of the service).
priority list
stoplist
ext
wipe list
landing
The format of the lists is semicolon-separated.
The landing page is given as is, UTF8 encoding.
Key - convenient for work through WinCrypt format (base64 wrapped if necessary).
POST /<cid>/<group>/stat
Sending statistics
The content of HTTP request body is application/x-www-form-urlencoded container with the following fields:
total - total files processed
crypted - total files encrypted
wiped - total files deleted
ignored - total files skipped
Statistics is sent every 15 minutes, the time is set as a constant in the program.
LANDING PAGE
There are two landing pages in total:
- on a computer
- on the Internet.
On the computer, next to the encrypted file is a text file with the same name and a different extension.
This file should contain a simple link to the page in the .onion domain and instructions on how to download the tor-browser.
For now, let's assume that the path to the landing page is the base .onion domain plus a path equal to md5(bot ID).
It's simple, and prevents brute force picking up pages of other targets.
If a tor-domain was specified in the target's properties, the page should open on that domain as well.
Automatic management of tor-domains - a separate topic.
You can prepare 100500 of them and assign them from the blanks.
To the landing page is a strict requirement to prohibit direct contact with the victim, except through our chat:
- everything that even slightly resembles a bitcoin address must be brutally rejected when loading page text into the admin,
and deleted when the page text is saved.
- Instead, the address for payments should be replaced by a macro.
Let's say, %PAYMENT_ADDRESS%.
- in the text should not get contacts of the admin (e-mail, jabber and all possible phones, messengers, IRC, etc.).
When updating the text of the page in the admin area should automatically check and reject pages with attempts to
to write contacts and wallets on them.
Probably, it is also possible to make some kind of pre-moderation, with approval of changes on pages by our system operators.
In general, you can make a customized landing page only for advanced users, and for the majority of users
make standard texts, with substitution of basic data from macros.
Macros:
%PAYMENT_ADDRESS% - bitcoin wallet for payment (taken from the target's profile)
%DOUBLE_DATE% - date of doubling the payout
%LANDING_URL% - link for the landing file - a basic URL to which the bot will glue its own id to get a full link to the landing page on the Internet.
The appearance of the pages is approximately as follows (competitor samples):
http://aplebzu47wgazapdqks6vrcv6zcnjppkbxbr6wketf56nf6aq2nmyoyd.onion/837F964AF6B77803#info
http://aplebzu47wgazapdqks6vrcv6zcnjppkbxbr6wketf56nf6aq2nmyoyd.onion/F32917DB2AA982C9#info
Do not plagiarize!
The meaning should be preserved:
- there should be information on how to buy bitcoins, a list of sites and exchanges
- chat login
- form for test decryption of the file.
Test file decryption can be used only once and only for image files,
as they usually do not carry important information.
When giving the decrypted result, you should check the file contents by signature.
If the signature of any of the allowed types does not match, do not give the content and write atata.
Allowed types: jpg, png, gif, bmp.
The page changes its appearance depending on the target's state:
- if the target has not paid and the next payment deadline has not expired, the page has a normal appearance
- if the target has not paid and the next payment period has expired, the page has a warning and a new amount (doubled every period).
- if the target has paid, there is a link to download an anchor.
IMPORTANT: the amount payable on the landing page is ALWAYS displayed without discount!
IMPORTANT: the visibility of the link to the anchor is ALWAYS controlled by the corresponding flag from the target's properties (see below - the target has a separate flag
"anlocker available" flag), NOT the PAYMENT FLAG!
IMPORTANT: the view of the landing pages can be controlled by commands from the chat after the PAYMENT FACT has been FIXED to our bitcoin address.
(it is necessary to have a transaction with the required amount to our assigned address from the target's profile).
Two things can change:
- switching the page between "not paid" and "paid" view and the corresponding availability of the link to the anlocker
- bitcoin address for payment.
All these actions are done by the recovery, only after you have paid us and we have recorded it.
BEFORE PAYMENT these actions are NOT available!
ADMINKA
There are several roles in the admin area (by admin area we mean not only the part of the system accessible to administrators, but also the web application in general):
- admin - sees everything, creates any accounts
- Advert - sees only his own data, does not see data of neighboring advert, sees chats by his bots and targets.
- victim - sees only his chat
ADMIN SECTION
This describes the list of pages visible only to the admin (page name and list of properties or information on it):
* Dashboard with statistics:
- total admins
- total bots
- bots active for the last 24 hours
- total payments
* System settings
- A list (pool) of free bitcoin addresses that can be assigned to the advert's targets.
Only those that have not yet been assigned to advert targets are available for modification.
This page will be read-only in the implementation with automatic clearing (see below).
* Account Management (Advert Profiles)
This page lists the adverts (accounts) with the following table columns:
- name
- commission (percentage)
- (per/at a time) block an advert
- edit addvert
And also the button
- create a new advert
Blocking an account means that the advert will no longer be able to log in to his account.
All payments to his wallet are suspended.
When creating a new advert, he is assigned a bitcoin address by the PROXY system, to which payments from victims are made.
This address is not visible to the advert himself.
In editing the profile of an advert, you can change the fields:
- name
- commission
- personal bitcoin-address of the advert (THIS IS NOT THE ADDRESS, which will be assigned to the target by us!).
This is the SECOND address, which is available for editing by both the admin and the advert.
For address assignments, see the "ADVERTISEMENTS" section.
- withdrawal via mixer
- mixer bitcoin address
- mixer time
- mixer fee
These fields need to be specified - depends on what features the bitmix API provides.
It is possible that the time and commission are not controlled by us, but are assigned by the mixer automatically.
Advanced statistics should also be visible for each advert
- total profit
- total bots
This stat should not be generated when opening the page, but should be loaded on a separate button/link.
(we do so because the statistic most likely makes heavy requests to the database).
ADVERTISEMENT SECTION
The list of pages visible to the advert is described here.
* Targets
The following fields are visible in the list:
- title
- group
- number of bots
- activity time of the last bot
- edit
- list of bots (a separate page opens, see below)
- delete/archive.
Also at the top of the page is the "Add" button.
You can only delete a target that has had 0 taps.
You can only send a target that had taps (i.e. the database has information about bots with the specified group) to the archive.
The archive is a separate section with targets with all the same information and only for viewing.
When creating/editing a goal there are the following fields:
** general
- name
- short name of the group, validated by the following rule:
a string of 6 characters, the first three are lower case Latin, the rest are numbers.
The group is a unique field, and is the target identifier in the system.
The "name" field is used inside the admin area
for easy display; the "group" field is used in various queries and interactions between parts of the system.
- status: paid/not paid (can be set both manually and automatically by payment scripts).
If there was a payment, we need to output a list of payments from the bitcoin network with links to the transaction
in any blockchain explorer.
That is, the operator should know if there was a payment or if the payment flag was set manually.
- chat nickname (operator by default)
** payment
- bitcoin address for payment (personal)
- bitcoin-address of the recovery (optional); its purpose - see CHATS section
- payout amount
- payout deadline, after which doubling starts (optional fields from here on)
- doubling interval, in days
- available discount (for recompanies), in percent (by default there is no discount, i.e. the value is 0).
Off by default.
See also under "CHATS" about disabling this option from chat.
** lending
- tor-domain (optional)
- Landing-text on the site (it is possible to make it an additional option, and by default use the standard text)
- landing-text in the file
- "anlocker available" - flag (checkbox) whether the anlocker on the landing page is available for downloading
- flag "test decryption available" (reset when test decryption is used)
** locker settings
- key type (private/group)
The only difference here is how many bots can use one key.
- private and public keys in human-understandable form
ATTENTION!
SEE ABOUT VALIDATION OF LENDING-TEXTS IN THE CORRESPONDING SECTION!!!!
- list of stopped services and processes
- list of priority directories
- list of ignored paths and masks
- list of working extensions
- list to delete
All lists are ; (semicolon) separated valid Windows path and file masks.
In addition, the target's properties store the private encryption key, the locker program, and the anti-locker program.
These properties are generated by the system when the target is created and are only available for viewing/downloading.
When a new target is created, a new bot build is generated for it and stored in the database/disk.
The following data is passed to the bot creation script:
- newly generated encryption key
- handle text for the file
- list of services and processes to be stopped
- list of priority directories
- list of ignored paths and masks
- list of working extensions
- list to delete
In response, the bot script creates two binaries - locker and anlocker.
The specified properties are flashed into these binaries.
If a separate tor-domain is specified for the target, you need to ensure that the landing page works through it.
Accordingly, you need to automate the creation and raising of such domains.
As an option - domains can be prepared in advance in the number of HHH pieces, and the user will simply select them from the pool
(or it will be assigned to him automatically).
* Bots
This is an information page with a list of bots for a specific selected target, sorted by date of last activity and fields:
- CID
- group
- target
- last activity
- chat
When you visit the bot's page, besides this information, the system information collected by the bot should also be available.
CHATS
You can enter the chat room in two ways:
- from the target/bot page in the admin (operator/advert)
- from the landing page (victim/recovery).
From the landing page, anyone with a link can enter the chat room.
The mode of operation when several people enter the chat room from different addresses or browsers is not prohibited.
The only condition is that the nicknames of such users must be different.
The nickname of the administrator in the chat room is taken from his settings.
The link to the chat is generated on the landing page in such a way that it cannot be guessed by brute force (i.e. an element of randomness is introduced).
The chat is tied to a goal:
- if the target is a group key, then each bot in the group goes to the same chat room
- if the target is a private key, then each bot is assigned to a separate chat room.
Adverts can only see their chats, admins can see everything.
When entering a chat room, the victim is presented with an invitation (like in IRC channels) and a
a message saying that you can type /help to view available commands.
The chat has the following commands:
/help
displays text about the private and clear commands with their descriptions.
The unpaid command is excluded from the prompt
/private
Makes the chat invisible to other chat participants on the victim's side (i.e. if more than one person is sitting in the chat on the victim's side).
sits in the chat on the victim's side).
From the admin/advert side it has no effect on anything - the chat history is fully visible.
/clear
Clearing the chat history from the victim's side.
From the admin/advert side has no effect on anything - the chat history is full.
/unpaid
Turns off the "link to anlocker available" flag.
This and subsequent commands are reported in chat by the advert, for the recovery company, after agreement on payment and discount has been reached.
no more // -Available ONLY after receiving payment to bitcoin-address of the target.- crossed out
The command is NOT available when setting the "paid" flag manually from the admin area!
The command turns off the flag "anlocker available" in the target's profile and changes the view of the landing page accordingly.
/bitcoin <address bitcoin>
Changes the address of the wallet for payment on the landing page.
Available ONLY after receiving payment to the target's bitcoin address.
COMMAND is NOT available when setting the "paid" flag manually from the admin!
/paid
Sets the "paid" flag.
ACCESSIBLE ONLY after receiving payment to the bitcoin address of the target.
COMMAND IS NOT ACCESSIBLE when setting the "paid" flag manually from the admin area!
The command enables the flag "anlocker available" in the target's profile and changes the view of the landing page accordingly.
The commands are intended for recavery actions only.
The general scenario is as follows:
- the recaver opens a private chat
- the recaverie introduces itself and negotiates a discount/payment terms
- in response, the operator from our side tells it the command to hide the fact of payment - /unpaid
- The recaverie uses the command /unpaid to disable the link to the anlocker and /bitcoin to change the bitcoin payment address on the landing page.
This must be done BEFORE payment!
- after confirming the payment, the operator will give the link to anlocker in private chat.
- after receiving payment from the victim to his/her address, the recavery can switch the page view to give the victim access to the anlocker.
CRIPTOVALUTES
We want to support other cryptocurrencies besides bitcoin - more
* dash
* zcash
* monero
Everything written in this ToR regarding bitcoin should be implemented for these cryptocurrencies as well.
For example, if in the properties of a record in the database it is required to specify the bitcoin address, then it is necessary to specify the addresses for the other currencies as well.
for the other currencies.
CLEARING AND PAYMENT SCRIPTS
Clearing is the settlement with adverts, paying them what is owed.
Depending on what clearing scheme will be chosen (manual or automatic),
the complexity and convenience of the system will vary.
Manual clearing:
+ is fully protected from wallet theft by staff and competitors
- requires additional daily work on distribution of funds
- requires additional manual work on system maintenance (address generation)
Automatic clearing:
+ you can completely automate everything
- your wallet is available to everyone who has access to the system (trust issue).
Purpose of payment scripts:
1) tracking bitcoin payments to our addresses
2) clearing (automatic sending of money to the addresses of adverts)
3) generating a pool of bitcoin addresses to assign to targets.
The first script will be in any clearing option; the second and third will be in automatic clearing only.
The scripts must be hosted and executed on a separate server with increased security.
The database is communicated with over the network.
AUTOMATIC CLEARING
If automatic clearing is to be done, then it is likely to be impossible to do without raising a full node of bitcoin.
On the payment server there is a wallet to which generated bitcoin-addresses are bound,
and a full bitcoin node is raised.
Clearing can be done either directly to the adverts' wallets or through mixers.
In case of working through mixers, in the general settings of the system you need to specify:
- option "clearing via mixer"
- mixer bitcoin address
- mixer time
- mixer commission
OR
the same options can be in the advert's profile (editable by admin).
There are three cron jobs:
1) checks incoming payments to each of the bitcoin addresses we have assigned to targets,
and marks the targets as paid if the amount is sufficient.
2) for paid targets, automatically generates outgoing payments to adverts' addresses
(incoming amount minus commission).
3) checks the stock of free addresses in the database, if necessary, generates new addresses and stores them in the database.
All this can be done in a lot of ways:
- a full bitcoin node has a bitcoin-cli utility that can do everything you need to do
- via JSON-RPC (lift on a full node)
MANUAL CLEARING
In this case you can do without a full bitcoin node.
1) To check the payment status of a bitcoin address, several approaches can be used:
- Use the native Bitcoin RPC API by connecting to a specific bitcoin server/s.
There is already an out of the box solution for PHP:
https://en.bitcoin.it/wiki/API_reference_%28JSON-RPC%29#PHP
https://en.bitcoin.it/wiki/Original_Bitcoin_client/API_calls_list
- Use the API of blockchain explorers.
This option is worse, as blockchain explorers change addresses, APIs and generally are prone to change.
addresses, APIs, and in general are subject to frequent changes.
For example:
https://blockchain.info/q
2) payments to adverts need to be processed manually.
To do this, you will need a report "To payment" in the admin, which should contain a list of addresses with amounts.
list of addresses with amounts.
The owner of the system downloads this report daily and manually makes payments.
3) In the same way, you need to manually generate new bitcoin addresses and upload them to the admin,
on a separate page in the system settings.
SECOND VERSION
The following needs to be provided:
a new loader is needed, which will load and run the load in batches (several .exe at once)
2. pwgrab styler should be adapted to dump data into storage:
- replace DPOST protocol with file transfer protocol.
- get the address of the command server via Emercoin DNS
- bot CID
- Bot CID should be generated uniformly and unambiguously, regardless of the user,
so that its generation can be repeated in the styler.
It is probably necessary to completely separate pwgrab from browsers engine,
cutting off all unnecessary stuff and making it minimalistic (remove left and duplicate code).
There should be a section in the admin to view merged passwords.
Binding to the advert; be sure to memorize the ID of the bot/machine.
Obtaining the address of the command server via Emercoin DNS.
